wikitext:
{{Chess Opening Theory/Position |Caro-Kann defence |eco=[[Chess/ECOB|B10-B19]] }} == 2. Nf3 == White develops a knight. This is a fairly unusual move sometimes played by Bobby Fischer. It usually is just a move-order difference. After Black plays [[/2...d5|'''2...d5''']] (which, as after 2. d4, is almost always the move) this usually transposes to the: * Exchange Caro-Kann, after 3. exd5 cxd5 4. d4 Nc6 (normally seen in the move order [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...c6/2. d4/2...d5/3. exd5/3...cxd5/4. Nf3/4...Nc6|2. d4 d5 3. exd5 cxd5 4. Nf3 Nc6]]). * Two knights attack, after 3. Nc3 (normally seen in the move order [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...c6/2. Nc3/2...d5/3. Nf3|2. Nc3 d5 3. Nf3]]). Two unique lines are 2...d5 3. exd5 cxd5 4. Ne5!?, the Apocalypse attack, and 2...d5 3. d3, offering to go into a queenless middlegame. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}} :'''1.e4 c6 2.Nf3'''
2 3 4 5
